stories.title: Texas 9, A&M 8 — Win Streak to 28, Game 2 Today at 1 PM CT

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
No. 1 Texas survived a wild one Friday — 9-8 over Texas A&M.

Win streak: 28 games. New program record for the Longhorns.

Game 2 is TODAY at 1 PM CT on ESPN2. The Aggies are not going quietly. 🥎

#HookEm #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Texas wins ugly 9-8 — and that might prove more than the blowouts do.

Any team can win when everything clicks. No. 1 Texas finds a way when it doesn't.

28 and counting. 🔥

#HookEm #NCAASoftball #SECSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
No. 1 Texas just made their fans sweat a little — and the win streak is still alive.

The Longhorns survived a wild 9-8 battle against Texas A&M on Friday, extending their program-record winning streak to 28 consecutive games. Texas is now 29-1 on the season. This isn't a blowout machine anymore — this is a team that finds a way to win even when things get messy, which might actually be the most reassuring thing Texas fans could see.

Let's be clear: Texas A&M is a real team. They pushed the No. 1 program in the country to the final out. But the Longhorns have Teagan Kavan (13-0) in the circle when they need her most, a lineup loaded with power (Reese Atwood at 11 HRs, Katie Stewart at 15), and a mentality that simply doesn't wilt.

Game 2 is today at 1 PM CT on ESPN2 in Austin. This series is very much alive.

Can the Aggies pull off the upset and snap the streak — or does Texas make it 29 in a row this afternoon?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 9:00 AM ET

```
Texas survives 9-8 — win streak hits 28 games and counting! Game 2 today at 1 PM CT on ESPN2. 🥎
```

stories.title: Arkansas 6, Florida 2 — Razorbacks Stun No. 3 Gators in Game 1

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
UPSET ALERT 🚨

No. 9 Arkansas beat No. 3 Florida 6-2 in Game 1 at Bogle Park.

Robyn Herron: 5 IP, 3 H, 8 K. Florida had a season-low 3 hits.

Florida must win BOTH Games 2 and 3.

#WPS #GoGators #NCAASoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Reagan Johnson broke the Arkansas career hits record with an RBI single Friday.

History made — against No. 3 Florida, in the same inning that put the game away. 👀

#WPS #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
The biggest upset of the weekend just happened in Fayetteville.

No. 9 Arkansas beat No. 3 Florida 6-2 on Friday night at Bogle Park, and it wasn't close. Razorbacks pitcher Robyn Herron was untouchable — five innings, three hits, eight strikeouts. Florida managed just three hits all game, a season low. Ace Keagan Rothrock (who entered at 17-2) was knocked out in a four-run Arkansas fifth inning.

And then there was Reagan Johnson. In that same fifth inning, the Arkansas centerfielder broke the school's all-time career hits record with an RBI single. History made, in a win over a top-3 opponent.

Arkansas now leads the series 1-0. Florida must win both Games 2 and 3 to take the series — and after a three-hit performance from Rothrock, Gators fans have real questions about this team's ability to hit top-tier pitching.

Does this change how you see Florida as a WCWS contender?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 11:00 AM ET

```
Arkansas stuns No. 3 Florida, 6-2. Herron dominates, Reagan Johnson breaks the career hits record. Razorbacks lead the series 1-0.
```

stories.title: Kendall Wells HR Watch — 25 HRs, 5 From Freshman Record

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells: 25 HR this season — leads the nation.

The NCAA freshman record is 30. She has 23+ games left.

Record holders: Jocelyn Alo, Lauren Chamberlain, Kelly Majam.

Wells is coming for it. 🔥

#BoomerSooner #NCAASoftball #Softball

facebook.long_form:
```
Kendall Wells is chasing softball history — and she's getting close.

The Oklahoma freshman catcher now has 25 home runs on the season, the most by any player in college softball. She sits just five home runs away from the NCAA freshman HR record of 30, a mark shared by three legends: Jocelyn Alo (OU, 2018), Lauren Chamberlain (OU, 2012), and Kelly Majam (Hawaii, 2010).

On Friday in Baton Rouge against No. 20 LSU, Wells didn't add to the HR total — but she still delivered a clutch 2-run single in the 7th inning to tie the game against LSU. She's the kind of player who impacts the game even when the home run isn't falling.

With 23-plus games remaining on the schedule, Wells has every opportunity to break that record. Oklahoma has also already broken the SEC's single-season team home run record — and is averaging nearly four team HRs per game.

What's your prediction — when does Wells hit No. 26?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 1:00 PM ET

```
25 HR. 5 from history. Kendall Wells is coming for the freshman record. 🥎
```

stories.title: Oklahoma 3, LSU 2 (8 inn.) — Sooners Stay Perfect in SEC

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma goes to extras in Baton Rouge — gets out alive, 3-2 over No. 20 LSU in 8 innings.

Miali Guachino: now 9-0.

Oklahoma in SEC play: 7-0. First year in the conference. 🥎

#BoomerSooner #SECSoftball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
Oklahoma is doing something no one expected — and they're making it look easy.

The Sooners won 3-2 in eight innings at No. 20 LSU on Friday, improving to 33-2 overall and 7-0 in SEC play. That's the best conference record in the league. In their first year as an SEC member.

LSU had the lead going into the seventh inning, and a raucous crowd at Tiger Park was starting to sense an upset. Then Kendall Wells stepped up with a 2-run single to tie the game. LSU tied it back up in the bottom of the seventh. And then in the eighth, Gabbie Garcia delivered the go-ahead RBI to put the Sooners ahead for good.

Miali Guachino got the win, improving to 9-0 on the season. Game 2 is today at 11 AM CT on SEC Network.

Oklahoma is 7-0 in SEC play in their first year in the conference. Is this the most dominant conference debut in recent softball history?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM ET

```
Oklahoma wins in extras — 3-2 over LSU. The Sooners are 7-0 in SEC play in their first year in the conference.
```

stories.title: NFCA Week 7 Rankings — Texas Holds No. 1, Full Top 10

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
NFCA Week 7 Top 10:

No. 1 Texas | No. 2 TTU | No. 3 Florida
No. 5 Oklahoma | No. 6 Alabama | No. 7 UCLA
No. 8 FSU | No. 9 Nebraska | No. 10 Arkansas

Duke and Washington both back in the Top 25. 👀

#NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball #SECSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
The Week 7 NFCA rankings are here, and the top of college softball looks exactly like you'd expect.

Texas holds the No. 1 spot, with Texas Tech at No. 2 and Florida at No. 3. Oklahoma checks in at No. 5, Alabama at No. 6, UCLA at No. 7, Florida State at No. 8, Nebraska at No. 9, and Arkansas at No. 10.

Two programs made their return this week: Duke is back at No. 24 after a two-week absence, and Washington re-entered at No. 25 — their first ranking since February 11 of last year.

The team that dropped out? Grand Canyon, which had been one of the feel-good stories of the season with an unbeaten start. Their first loss ended their Top 25 stay.

Which team do you see winning it all in Oklahoma City this June?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 5:30 PM ET

```
NFCA Week 7 is out. Texas still No. 1. Duke and Washington are back in. Who's winning the WCWS?
```

stories.title: Alabama Combined Perfect Game — 11th in Program History

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Alabama threw a COMBINED perfect game Wednesday.

Briski. Johnson. Hodges.

Three pitchers. Zero baserunners. The 11th perfect game in Bama program history. 🔥

#RollTide #CollegeSoftball #Softball

facebook.long_form:
```
Alabama just threw their 11th perfect game in program history — and they did it with three different pitchers.

On Wednesday, Jocelyn Briski, Alea Johnson, and Braya Hodges combined to retire every single North Alabama batter they faced. No hits. No walks. No baserunners.

There's something especially impressive about a combined perfect game. You're not just asking one pitcher to be flawless — you're asking every pitcher who enters to be flawless. That's a testament to the entire pitching staff, their conditioning, and the defense behind them.

Alabama sits at 29-2 and is one of the most complete programs in the country. Eleven perfect games. Let that sink in.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:00 PM ET

```
11th perfect game in program history. Alabama's pitching staff is something special. 🔥
```

stories.title: Oklahoma State Loses Melina Wilkison for Season (Injury)

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Tough news for Oklahoma State.

Melina Wilkison — .326 avg, 7 HR, 22 SB — is out for the rest of 2026.

One of the most complete players in the Big 12, gone for the year.

Heal up, Melina. 🥎

#GoPokes #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
```
Oklahoma State received difficult news Friday: outfielder Melina Wilkison is done for the 2026 season.

Wilkison, a transfer from Indiana, had been one of the Pokes' most dynamic contributors — batting .326 with 7 home runs and 22 stolen bases. That combination of power and speed is exceptionally rare. Losing her is a genuine blow to Oklahoma State's Big 12 title aspirations.

The announcement came on March 27. No specific injury timetable has been confirmed.

Heal up, Melina. Get back to full strength, and come back stronger next year.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:30 PM ET

```
Tough news: Oklahoma State's Melina Wilkison (.326, 7 HR, 22 SB) is out for the season. Wishing her a full recovery.
```
